3d_model.notes: Dispersed pavilion massing on 15,000sqm site — 8 volumes of varying height arranged to maximize sea views from all guest rooms. Massing respects 15m stream setback; northeast pavilion (spa) repositioned 18m from stream centerline.

renders.notes: 5 perspectives: aerial site overview, entry sequence, restaurant pavilion at sunset, infinity pool from guest room terrace, spa courtyard. All views demonstrate material palette and landscape integration.

material_direction.notes: Raw concrete, local stone (piedra volcánica from BCS quarry), weathered teak, and white plaster — restrained palette responding to the East Cape landscape. No paint colors — all natural materials. Solar panels integrated as a design element on south-facing roofs.

color_direction.notes: Monochromatic — concrete grays and stone taupes, with warm teak accents. Landscape planting (native xeriscape) introduces subtle greens. No applied color — all tones derived from natural materials.

space_arrangement.notes: Dispersed pavilion plan: guest room clusters (3 groups of 5) on upper terrace, restaurant and reception in central arrival pavilion, infinity pool on western promontory with full sea view, spa in northeast pavilion (repositioned for stream setback compliance). All 2,000sqm program areas confirmed in schematic plan.

review_notes: Concept fully addresses the approved 2,000sqm program across all 8 space types. The dispersed pavilion typology is particularly well-suited to the 15,000sqm site and the hydrology constraint — the northeast spa placement now exceeds the 15m CONAGUA setback. All five deliverables complete. Recommend approval for advancement to architectural design development.
